rulings.Case Title: MAGGIE S. VS. CALIFRONIA PHYSICIANS' SERVICE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PHYSICIANS' SERVICE DBA BLUE SHIELD OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, November 13, 2024, Line 1, 1- DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PHYSICIANS' SERVICE DBA BLUE SHIELD OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Defendant California Physicians' Service dba Blue Shield of California's (Blue Shield) motion for summary judgment, or in the alternative, summary adjudication is granted. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: MAGGIE S. VS. CALIFRONIA PHYSICIANS' SERVICE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEFENDANT HUMAN AFFAIRS INTERNATIONAL OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, November 13, 2024, Line 1, 2- DEFENDANT HUMAN AFFAIRS INTERNATIONAL OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Defendant Human Affairs International of California's (HAI) motion for summary judgment, or in the alternative, summary adjudication is granted. The first inquiry for a breach of contract or breach of covenant of good faith and fair dealing claim is whether the parties have entered into a contract. Plaintiff offers no evidence that she had any contract with HAI, so the claims fail. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES ADAM NUNZIATO, AN INDIVIDUAL VS. ROBERT J. KORMAN, AS AN INDIVIDUAL AND TRUSTEE OF ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of August 29, 2024 Void Orders Denying Defendants Motion To Strike And Demurrer To Plaintiffs Complaint For Being "Untimely"; Mpa And Affidavit Of Robert J. Korman
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, November 13, 2024, Line 9, DEFENDANTS ROBERT J. KORMAN, AS AN INDIVIDUAL AND TRUSTEE OF THE KORMAN FAMILY IRREVOCABLE TRUST, and NANCY E. RYTI's Motion For Reconsideration Of August 29, 2024 Void Orders Denying Defendants Motion To Strike And Demurrer To Plaintiffs Complaint For Being "Untimely". Defendants' "motion for reconsideration of August 29, 2024 void orders denying defendants' motion to strike and demurrer to plaintiff's complaint for being 'untimely'" is denied. None of the "new and different facts" asserted in support of the motion change the fact that defendants' motion to strike and demurrer were untimely filed and served. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, November 13, 2024, Line 10, 2- PLAINTIFF EMA BELL's Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment. Plaintiff Emma Bell's motion to approve Proposition 65 settlement and consent judgment is granted. The warning required by the settlement is compliant, the award of attorney's fees is reasonable, and the penalty amount is reasonable. (Health & Safety Code 25249.7(f)(4).) rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, November 13, 2024, Line 11, DEFENDANT ONERENT INC. DBA POPLAR HOMES' Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment: And Quash Service Of Summons (CCP Section 418.10). Transferred to be heard in department 501 on November 27, 2024, at 9:30 a.m. That department handles all cases arising from the acquisition of real property per SF Local Rule 8.10A1. Parties to comply with SFLR 2.7B and deliver courtesy copies of their respective filings in support of and in opposition to the motion to department 501 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date and department no later than November 15, 2024. =(302/RBU)
